children.heading: Workforce development systems and investment activities
children.label: a Workforce development systems and investment activities
children.text: The Secretary of Labor and the Secretary of Education shall take such actions as the Secretaries determine to be appropriate to provide for the orderly transition from any authority under the Workforce Investment Act of 1998 ( et seq.) to any authority under part A of subchapter I. Such actions shall include the provision of guidance related to unified State planning, combined State planning, and the performance accountability system described in such part.29 U.S.C. 2801

children.heading: Workforce investment activities
children.label: b Workforce investment activities
children.text: The Secretary of Labor shall take such actions as the Secretary determines to be appropriate to provide for the orderly transition from any authority under the Workforce Investment Act of 1998 to any authority under parts B through E of subchapter I.

children.heading: Adult education and literacy programs
children.label: c Adult education and literacy programs
children.text: The Secretary of Education shall take such actions as the Secretary determines to be appropriate to provide for the orderly transition from any authority under the Adult Education and Family Literacy Act ( et seq.), as in effect on the day before , to any authority under the Adult Education and Family Literacy Act, as amended by this Act.20 U.S.C. 9201July 22, 2014

children.heading: Employment services activities
children.label: d Employment services activities
children.text: The Secretary of Labor shall take such actions as the Secretary determines to be appropriate to provide for the orderly transition from any authority under the Wagner-Peyser Act ( et seq.), as in effect on the day before , to any authority under the Wagner-Peyser Act, as amended by this Act.29 U.S.C. 49July 22, 2014

children.heading: Vocational rehabilitation programs
children.label: e Vocational rehabilitation programs
children.text: The Secretary of Education and the Secretary of Health and Human Services shall take such actions as the Secretaries determine to be appropriate to provide for the orderly transition from any authority under the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 ( et seq.), as in effect on the day before , to any authority under the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, as amended by this Act.29 U.S.C. 701July 22, 2014

children.heading: Proposed regulations
children.label: 1 Proposed regulations
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary of Labor, the Secretary of Education, and the Secretary of Health and Human Services, as appropriate, shall develop and publish in the Federal Register proposed regulations relating to the transition to, and implementation of, this Act (including the amendments made by this Act).July 22, 2014

children.heading: Final regulations
children.label: 2 Final regulations
children.text: Not later than 18 months after , the Secretaries described in paragraph (1), as appropriate, shall develop and publish in the Federal Register final regulations relating to the transition to, and implementation of, this Act (including the amendments made by this Act).July 22, 2014

children.heading: Expenditure of funds during transition
children.label: g Expenditure of funds during transition

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2) and in accordance with regulations developed under subsection (f), States, grant recipients, administrative entities, and other recipients of financial assistance under the Workforce Investment Act of 1998 may expend funds received under such Act in order to plan and implement programs and activities authorized under this Act.

children.heading: Additional requirements
children.label: 2 Additional requirements
children.text: Not more than 2 percent of any allotment to any State from amounts appropriated under the Workforce Investment Act of 1998 for fiscal year 2014 may be made available to carry out activities authorized under paragraph (1) and not less than 50 percent of any amount used to carry out activities authorized under paragraph (1) shall be made available to local entities for the purposes of the activities described in such paragraph.
